T4.analytic: W=(1+r)s'-r's=0 => s=lam(1+r) in Frac(k[y]); det const term (1+r)(1+q'-lam p')=1 => 1+r unit => r=0 (NF); then s=lam, q'=lam p'. Tame: shear o E_y.

theorem: Plane Keller maps of N=1 shape (deg_x <= 1 in both components) are exactly the tame family shear o E_y: r=0, s=lambda, q=lambda*p. deg_x(f)>=2 pure-x-power terms never Keller. Exotic single and pair monoms never Keller. Elementary any degree invertible. Leading Jac vanishes; pure-power axis forms work; non-pure binary leadings alone never Keller.
gap_to_full_plane_jc: Remaining: (i) every plane Keller map has pure-power leading for ALL degrees (classical n=2; machine-forced for g=y axis samples); (ii) after pure-power + GL2, multi-mixed support in axis shape cancels only when all mixed coeffs vanish (sealed single+pair; full multi open for unbounded support); (iii) Jung-van der Kulk lifts tame => auto. Items (i)+(ii) for all d are the classical content of plane JC; this certificate closes the N=1 and single/pair-exotic algebraic core with no degree cap.
